commands.repair_reason: Protected security work and the suppression-visibility policy need secops review; the narrow code defect is clear, but automation should not decide the accepted-risk UX.; Needs attention: The diff changes audit visibility and currently lets suppression config hide its own disclosure marker.; Review finding: [P2] Keep suppression usage visible — src/security/audit.ts:225-236 (sha=176a61f16e56e2fd04ffae82a286cf36f669a524)
commands.review_summary:
Adds typed `security.audit.suppressions` config, filters matched audit findings into `suppressedFindings`, updates CLI/docs/changelog output, and approval-gates agent shell edits to suppressions outside yolo mode.

Reproducibility: yes. for the review finding by source inspection: a suppression targeting the dangerous-flag finding for `security.audit.suppressions configured (<count>)` would pass through the generic filter and remove the disclosure from active output. This is a feature PR rather than a runtime bug report, so I did not run a live audit command in the read-only checkout.
commands.review_followup: Protected security work and the suppression-visibility policy need secops review; the narrow code defect is clear, but automation should not decide the accepted-risk UX.
